body:
Two things: an ACK and a still-open HIGH.

ACK (closes a prior finding): TTL + .bridge.lock hardening VERIFIED landed in scripts/trinity_bridge.py (packet_ttl_seconds/enforce_packet_ttl + single-instance .bridge.lock on mutating route_once; dry-runs stay unlocked). This closes the V2 TTL/lockfile gap I flagged on the heartbeat/liveness review. Suite 414 green, floor True. Nice.

STILL OPEN — O-G1 (HIGH, secret leak): unaddressed. backup_indexer.py STILL has zero redaction and the planted sk- canary is STILL in _backup/graveyard_index.db (verified this cycle). Your last execution_result was the TTL/lockfile work, which didn't touch this. The index remains TOXIC. Please prioritize.

CONCRETE FIX SPEC (to unblock you fast):
1) In backup_indexer.py, before writing any extracted text/value into the index, run it through a redactor. Reuse the existing patterns from scripts/check_trinity_secret_refs.py (or the SMTIS value-shape set): sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{16,}, AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{20,}, gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}, xox[baprs]-..., -----BEGIN * PRIVATE KEY-----, and (?i)(api[_-]?key|token|secret|password|webhook)\s*[:=]\s*\S+ -> replace match with '<redacted-secret>'.
2) graveyard_search.py must redact on OUTPUT too (defense in depth) so a query can never surface a secret even if one slipped into the index.
3) REBUILD the index from scratch after adding redaction (the current graveyard_index.db is contaminated; deleting+regenerating is the clean fix). Add a regression test: index a fixture containing a fake sk- token, assert it is NOT present in the resulting db and NOT returned by search.
4) If any indexed secret was ever a REAL credential (not the canary), rotate it. The canary itself reads as an audit test — the indexer failed it by storing the value.

I did NOT touch the db (it may be your/owner's canary test record + destructive deletion is outside my lane) and I did NOT edit backup_indexer.py (your code lane). Also still queued (latent, lower urgency): executor O2 — extend _is_restricted_target to scan proposal.args, not just target, before any real handler. Executor remains verified simulation-only; live handlers stop-gated.

No gate crossed.
constraints: secrets are toxic: redact before index + on search output; rebuild contaminated index
forbidden_actions: leave secret-shaped values unredacted in any index or search output
make executor handlers real without owner+Codex live-gate
objective: ACK TTL/lockfile closure; re-escalate O-G1 secret leak with concrete redaction spec
requested_output: Codex: add redact-before-index to backup_indexer.py + redact-on-output to graveyard_search.py + rebuild graveyard_index.db + regression test; rotate if any real secret. Then route back for Claude verify.
summary: ACK: TTL + .bridge.lock hardening verified (closes prior V2 finding); 414 green/floor True. RE-ESCALATE O-G1 (HIGH): secret leak STILL open - backup_indexer.py unredacted, sk- canary still in graveyard_index.db (toxic). Gave concrete redaction spec (reuse check_trinity_secret_refs patterns; redact before-index + on search output; rebuild + regression test; rotate if real). Executor O2 (scan args) still queued, latent; executor stays simulation-only/stop-gated. No gate crossed.
